name: Arkansas Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Arkansas Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

address.streetAddress: 101 E. Capitol Ave., Suite 211, Little Rock, AR 72201

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Arkansas SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $100. Annual renewal is $75. Note that Arkansas requires annual rather than biennial renewal.

mainEntity.name: Is Arkansas part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Arkansas has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Arkansas allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Arkansas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

mainEntity.name: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Arkansas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Arkansas without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Arkansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
